name: Increase the number of weekly active users to drive business growth

meta.alignment: Directly supports the company mission by enabling more teams to work effectively together, and aligns with the product vision of becoming the single source of truth for remote and hybrid teams

children.name: I want to easily find what I need when I log in, without feeling overwhelmed by information

meta.evidence: I open it first thing in the morning and immediately get confused about where to pick up from yesterday.

children.name: If we build a personalized dashboard, then all users will quickly find what they need when they log in because it will show them relevant information from where they left off yesterday

meta.experiment: Create a clickable prototype of the dashboard and conduct A/B testing with 10% of the user base
meta.signal: Increase in time spent on the platform within the first 10 minutes of login, decrease in bounce rate

children.name: If we implement a 'continue from yesterday' feature, then all users will easily pick up from where they left off because it will provide a clear call-to-action

meta.experiment: Conduct a fake door test by adding a 'continue from yesterday' button that doesn't actually work, and measure click-through rates
meta.signal: High click-through rate on the fake button, positive user feedback in surveys

children.name: If we provide a daily summary email, then all users will be more likely to log in and engage with the platform because they will be reminded of their progress and tasks

meta.experiment: Send a daily summary email to 20% of the user base for one week, and measure open rates, click-through rates, and subsequent login rates
meta.signal: Increase in open rates, click-through rates, and login rates among the test group compared to the control group

children.name: I need a simple way to get started with the product, with clear guidance on the first steps

meta.evidence: Honestly, confusing. I signed up, landed on a blank dashboard, and had no idea what to do.

children.name: If we build an interactive onboarding tour, then new users will complete their first task within 5 minutes because they will have clear guidance on the first steps

meta.experiment: Create a clickable prototype of the onboarding tour and measure the completion rate of the first task within 5 minutes
meta.signal: Increase in the percentage of new users completing their first task within 5 minutes

children.name: If we provide a curated list of getting-started resources, then new users will engage with the product for at least 10 minutes because they will have a clear understanding of the product's capabilities

meta.experiment: Create a fake door test by adding a 'Getting Started' button that links to a curated list of resources and measure the click-through rate and subsequent engagement time
meta.signal: Increase in the average engagement time of new users

children.name: If we offer personalized product recommendations based on user interests, then new users will explore at least 3 features within the first week because they will be more likely to find value in the product

meta.experiment: Conduct an A/B test by offering personalized product recommendations to a subset of new users and measuring the feature exploration rate
meta.signal: Increase in the percentage of new users exploring at least 3 features within the first week

children.name: If we send a series of guided email notifications, then new users will complete their profile and start using the product within 3 days because they will receive timely reminders and guidance

meta.experiment: Create an email campaign with guided notifications and measure the profile completion rate and subsequent product usage
meta.signal: Increase in the percentage of new users completing their profile and starting to use the product within 3 days

children.name: I struggle to navigate the product and find specific features without excessive clicking

meta.evidence: The navigation. I know the feature is there somewhere but I can't find it without clicking through three menus.

children.name: If we build a prominent search bar, then all users will quickly find specific features without excessive clicking because it reduces navigation complexity

meta.experiment: Create a clickable prototype with a search bar and measure user engagement through a usability testing session with 10 participants
meta.signal: Increase in successful feature discovery rate by 20% within the test group

children.name: If we implement a feature suggestion dropdown on the main navigation, then all users will easily find specific features without navigating through multiple menus because it provides immediate access to frequently used features

meta.experiment: Conduct an A/B test with 1000 users where 50% see the feature suggestion dropdown and measure the difference in click-through rates to specific features
meta.signal: 25% reduction in average clicks to reach a specific feature for the test group

children.name: If we add a 'Recently Used' section to the main dashboard, then all users will quickly access their most frequently used features without excessive navigation because it prioritizes their most common actions

meta.experiment: Run a fake door test by adding a 'Recently Used' button that doesn't actually work yet and measure the click-through rate on this button over a week
meta.signal: At least 30% of users click on the 'Recently Used' button, indicating demand for quick access to frequently used features

children.name: I want to have a clear overview of my team's work and progress in one place

meta.evidence: There's no single view that shows me my whole team. I have to go into each person's profile or each project separately.

children.name: If we build a team dashboard, then Managers will regularly check the platform because they can see their team's work and progress in one place

meta.experiment: Create a clickable prototype of the team dashboard and conduct usability testing with 5 managers
meta.signal: Increase in weekly active users by 15% within 2 weeks of launching the dashboard

children.name: If we provide a customizable team overview page, then Managers will be more likely to use the platform daily because they can prioritize their team's tasks and projects

meta.experiment: Conduct an A/B test with 20% of managers, where the treatment group has access to the customizable team overview page
meta.signal: 25% increase in daily active users among the treatment group within 1 week

children.name: If we send weekly team progress summaries to managers, then Managers will be more engaged with the platform because they receive regular updates on their team's work

meta.experiment: Design a fake door test where managers can opt-in to receive weekly team progress summaries, and measure the number of opt-ins
meta.signal: 20% of managers opt-in to receive the weekly summaries within 1 week

children.name: If we integrate a team performance metrics section into the dashboard, then Managers will spend more time on the platform because they can track their team's performance in real-time

meta.experiment: Conduct a usability testing session with 3 managers to validate the team performance metrics section
meta.signal: Average session duration increases by 30% among managers within 2 weeks of launching the team performance metrics section

children.name: I need to be able to quickly understand what's changed since I last logged in, without having to manually search for updates

meta.evidence: A team digest. Show me what changed since I last logged in. Bullet points. Who moved what, what got completed, what's newly blocked.

children.name: If we build a 'Since You Last Logged In' dashboard, then Managers will regularly log in to check updates because it saves them time and effort

meta.experiment: Create a clickable prototype of the dashboard and share it with 50 managers, asking for feedback and usage intent
meta.signal: Increase in weekly active users among the test group, with at least 30% reporting they would use the feature daily

children.name: If we send a weekly team digest email with updates since last login, then Managers will feel more connected to their team's progress because they're informed without extra effort

meta.experiment: Design and send a fake team digest email to 100 managers, measuring open rates, click-through rates, and feedback responses
meta.signal: At least 40% of recipients open the email, with 20% clicking through to the platform, and positive feedback from 30% of respondents

children.name: If we implement an in-app notification system highlighting changes since last login, then Managers will be more likely to engage with the platform regularly because they're notified of relevant updates

meta.experiment: Develop a minimal version of the notification system, testing it with 20 managers, and observing their engagement patterns over two weeks
meta.signal: An increase of at least 25% in the number of days managers log in per week, with at least 80% of them interacting with the notifications
